## What This Book Is Trying to Do

This book is not trying to replace the whole of physics with a slogan. It is trying to stage a framework carefully enough that its strengths and weaknesses can become visible.

That means beginning with primitive terms, conservation structure, and mappings, rather than jumping immediately to interpretation or applications. It means asking first whether the proposed starting point can support a coherent formal backbone. Only after that does it make sense to ask what the framework implies for more familiar domains such as relativity, gravity, quantum structure, or cosmology.

The book therefore proceeds in layers. It begins with foundations, then moves outward toward broader correspondences and applications. Along the way, it tries to keep a disciplined distinction between what is genuinely derived, what is only shown to correspond in a limited regime, what is interpretive, and what remains speculative.

The aim is not simply to present an alternative vocabulary. The aim is to see whether a different order of explanation produces a framework that is structurally cleaner, conceptually sharper, and more resonant with the actual geometry and behavior of physical systems.

In that sense, the book is better read as a structured test than as a finished doctrine. It does not ask for early loyalty. It asks to be followed far enough that its wager can be judged.
